channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé stars as France breeze past Iraq despite weather chaos
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé stars as France breeze past Iraq despite weather chaos
item.description: France defeated Iraq 3-0 in Philadelphia to mathematically secure their passage to the knockout stages. Kylian Mbappé scored twice and Ousmane Dembélé added a third, but the match was overshadowed by severe thunderstorms that forced a two hour and 12 minute delay, evacuation of spectators, and a complete washout of the pitch.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Romania parliament rejects liberal PM-designate Adrian Vestea
item.description: Romania's parliament rejected prime minister-designate Adrian Vestea on Monday, extending months of political turmoil in the EU and NATO member bordering Ukraine and raising the prospect of a snap election if a second government nominee also fails to secure lawmakers' backing.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: France-Iraq match suspended as stadium is evacuated due to thunderstorms
item.description: Heavy rain and an approaching thunderstorm delayed the second half of France's World Cup match against Iraq on Monday, forcing thousands of fans to seek shelter in the first weather-related disruption of the tournament. The stoppage came as severe weather also threatened another fixture in nearby New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Vance says UN nuclear inspectors will return to Iran as US suspends sanctions
item.description: The United States temporarily eased sanctions on Iranian oil on Monday after Vice President JD Vance said Tehran had agreed to allow UN nuclear inspectors back into the country, marking a significant step in renewed negotiations aimed at curbing Iran's nuclear programme and stabilising the Strait of Hormuz.
item.source: © Urs Flueeler, Pool Photo via AP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Rubio heads to UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain to discuss Iran deal with Gulf allies
item.description: Starting Tuesday, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is slated to visit three Gulf allies that were attacked by Iran during the US and Israeli war: the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Bahrain. Rubio appears to have his work cut out for him. The US’s Gulf allies were not warned in advance of “Operation Epic Fury” and now, many of them are being asked to invest in Iran’s reconstruction. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ethiopian PM's party secures win in national election
item.description: In tonight's edition, Ethiopia's ruling Prosperity Party takes roughly 90% of seats in elections that hand Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed another half decade in power. Also, in Tunisia, inflation topped 5 percent last month causing price hikes in food and essentials across the board. And Cape Verde keeps on coming after its two-all draw against two-time World Cup champions Uruguay.
item.dc:creator: Georja CALVIN-SMITH

item.title: ‘Messi is the enemy of Allah’: World Cup hoax falsely blames Algerian fans for chant
item.description: Lionel Messi is now the all-time top scorer in World Cup history, after scoring twice Monday against Austria in the 2026 World Cup. His first match, where he scored a hat-trick against Algeria, saw some online controversy. According to internet users, Algerian fans allegedly targeted him with an aggressive chant, calling Messi the "enemy of Allah" and threatening Argentina. But the audio is manipulated, and an old Arab protest chant against al-Sisi.
item.dc:creator: Vedika BAHL

item.title: Turnover at 10 Downing Street raises questions about 'whether Britain is indeed governable'
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigned on Monday, paving the way for Britain to have its seventh prime minister in just over a decade. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Martin Farr, Senior Lecturer in Contemporary British History at Newcastle University, says that the frequency of changes at 10 Downing Street since the Brexit vote "is very concerning and we're having many more questions about whether Britain is indeed governable".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Messi makes history as Argentina dominate Austria
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Messi makes history as Argentina dominate Austria
item.description: Argentina defeated Austria 2-0 in Arlington, Texas, with Lionel Messi scoring twice to become the all-time leading goal-scorer in World Cup history. The 38-year-old Argentine legend broke Miroslav Klose's record of 16 goals, cementing his status as one of football's greatest players. With two victories from two matches, Argentina have mathematically secured their passage to the last 32.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Shooting in Montreal Jewish neighbourhood leaves three dead, including suspect
item.description: A shooting in a Jewish neighbourhood in Montreal on Monday left three people dead, including the suspected shooter, local police said. A police officer and an unidentified resident were also killed.
item.source: © Christopher Katsarov, The Canadia via AP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Will Colombia's right-wing hardliner De la Espriella be able to govern?
item.description: Colombia on Sunday elected 47-year-old hardliner and millionaire lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella as the country’s next president just four years after electing Gustavo Petro the first left-wing president in the country's history. De la Espriella won the majority vote and received support from right-wingers in the US – but given his outsider status, will he be able to enact his policies?
item.dc:creator: Diya GUPTA

item.title: France - Iraq: Mbappé shines as Bleus demolish Iraq to advance
item.description: France faced Iraq in a crucial Group I clash at Philadelphia's stadium, but severe thunderstorms could potentially force a postponement of kick-off. The French, fresh from a commanding 3-1 victory over Senegal, secured a place in the last 32 with a 3-0 win against the Lions. Relive our live coverage.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA

item.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
item.description: Ukraine has intensified its drone attacks on the Russian-controlled territory of Crimea, prompting a fuel shortage on the peninsula. The campaign has prompted officials there to halt gasoline sales to the public and has caused a larger threat to Russia's fleet in the Black Sea. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis has the story.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Labour's last chance? Burnham set to replace Starmer as UK faces far-right surge
item.description: Another post-Brexit prime minister bites the dust. Keir Starmer owning up to the obvious, that he's too unpopular to lead the Labour Party into the next general election. After replacing the hard left's Jeremy Corbyn, the same Starmer won a whopping landslide two years ago on the promise of stability after all the chaos of Tory rule. Where did it all go wrong? Can the heavy favorite to replace him win back disgruntled Labour voters who first flocked to the Conservatives and now to the far-right Reform party?
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: US-Iran peace deal 'won't even come close to the expansive detailed Obama agreement'
item.description: Nadia Massih welcomes Larry Sabato, Director of the University of Virginia Center for Politics. JD Vance is hailing "good progress" on US-Iran peace talks, following President Trump's threats to hit Iran "harder than ever before". Sabato reminds us that this war is "almost universally seen as a fiasco" in the US. Seventy percent of Americans are against the war, which is unprecedented. Sabato also offers a scathing assessment of Trump's ever-changing war strategy: "The president cannot stick to a script, and he cannot stick to a prearranged strategy". And so he warns that "it's going to be a long 60 days!"
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: ‘The dad is useless’: Anger grows over French journalist’s Doku criticism
item.description: When Belgium star Jeremy Doku said he wanted to leave the World Cup to attend the birth of his son, it drew a furious reaction from French sports TV presenter France Pierron, who labelled childbirth “disgusting” and said fathers being there was “completely useless”. Her comments have since provoked outrage on social media and elicited support for Doku from fellow footballers.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: As Cuba's power crisis deepens, Havana residents speak out
item.description: Cubans are in the grips of a deepening power and fuel crisis – exacerbated by a US oil blockade launched earlier this year – that has left the country's poorest struggling to cope. FRANCE 24's exclusive report found that residents in Havana are now turning against their own government.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Afro B celebrates his Ivorian roots on 'Mapouka'
item.description: In this edition of our arts24 music show, Jennifer Ben Brahim chats with British Afrobeats superstar Afro B. He is the brains behind the smash hit "Drogba (Joanna)", one of first Afrobeats songs to break the US charts. He has collaborated over the years with the likes of French Montana and Chris Brown. He is back with a new single called "Mapouka", a love letter to Ivory Coast. It’s the first taste of his upcoming album, "Afro Ivoire".
item.dc:creator: Jennifer BEN BRAHIM

item.title: French court refuses trial on use of toxic insecticide in French West Indies
item.description: A French court has ruled against a trial on the use of a known toxic insecticide in the French West Indies, shutting down the possibility of an investigation in a health scandal that has been ongoing for 20 years. Despite being labelled as possibly carcinogenic in 1979, chlordécone was used between 1972 until 1993 - thanks to a by-law that allowed it to circumvent its ban, introduced in 1990. FRANCE 24's Shirli Sitbon tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US hails 'good progress' with Iran after first round of talks concludes
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance has said that a first round of US-Iran talks in Switzerland had laid a good foundation for reaching a final deal on ending the Middle East war that the US and Israel began in February. Vance's comments came after he met with Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf on June 21 and 22. The mediation effort in Switzerland had rocky moments, but the talks also led to some agreements between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: How can France adapt to recurrent heatwaves?
item.description: France is seeing a second consecutive heatwave in less than a month. Half the country is now under red alert as temperatures are expected to rise to 43°C in the southwestern city of Bordeaux and 39°C in the capital Paris. According to weather service Méteo-France, out of 51 heatwaves recorded nationwide since 1947, two-thirds have occurred since 2000 and about half of those episodes in the last 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Valérie DEKIMPE

item.title: A look back at Starmer's two years in power following his resignation
item.description: Britain's Keir Starmer has announced he's stepping down. The prime minister forced to resign after last month's loses in local elections. Starmer is expected to stay in the role until a new Labour party leader is picked. He is now set to become the country's shortest-serving Labour PM in history.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
